Title: Biosynthesis of phosphatidylglycerol in photosynthetic organisms

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces phosphatidic acid from DAG in a newly proposed ER pathway preferentially feeding PG biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGK2 participates with DGK4 in an ER-associated pathway that phosphorylates DAG to PA. 2. This PA is proposed to preferentially feed PG biosynthesis. 3. Reduced DGK2/DGK4 function impairs pollen germination/tube growth and specifically affects PG labeling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial microRNA suppression; radioactive pulse-chase labeling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double mutant is gametophyte lethal; leaky suppression causes slight dwarfism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in precursor supply rather than direct PG synthesis step

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces phosphatidic acid from DAG in a new pathway proposed to specifically supply PG biosynthesis in the ER
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGK4 acts with DGK2 to provide PA from DAG. 2. The generated PA preferentially enters PG biosynthesis. 3. Impairment of DGK2/DGK4 disrupts gametophyte function and reduces PG-specific radiolabeling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial microRNA suppression; radioactive pulse-chase labeling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double mutant is gametophyte lethal; suppression lines show slightly dwarfed growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as part of a newly proposed precursor-supply route for ER PG synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers CMP from CTP to phosphatidic acid to form CDP-diacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthesis and chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with CDS5 to support plastid PG synthesis; double loss decreases PG and impairs chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDS4 is a plastid-localized prokaryotic-type CDS in Arabidopsis. 2. cds4 single mutants are near wild type, indicating redundancy. 3. cds4 cds5 double mutants show decreased PG and pale-green chloroplast defects, supporting its role in plastid PG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization of fluorescent fusion proteins; T-DNA mutant analysis; lipid profiling of cds4 cds5 double mutant
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced PG in combination with cds5 causes pale-green phenotype and retarded growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with CDS5; no 16:1(Δ3t)-containing PG formed in cds4 cds5 double mutant

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers CMP from CTP to phosphatidic acid to form CDP-diacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthesis and chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with CDS4 to maintain plastid PG synthesis; loss with cds4 reduces PG and impairs thylakoid development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDS5 is plastid localized and contributes to CDP-DAG formation for plastid PG synthesis. 2. Single mutants show little phenotype, but cds4 cds5 double mutants have reduced PG and defective chloroplast development. 3. Plastid PG synthesis by CDS4/CDS5 is required for 16:1(Δ3t)-containing PG accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization of fluorescent fusion proteins; T-DNA mutant analysis; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plastid PG with cds4 causes pale-green phenotype and retarded growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice cds5 mutant mentioned for PA signaling, but review emphasis for PG metabolism is Arabidopsis plastid role

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms CDP-diacylglycerol from phosphatidic acid and CTP in mitochondria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for mitochondrial function and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for mitochondrial PG and CL biosynthesis; knockout abolishes CL accumulation and causes lethal growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis CDS6 is a Tam41 homolog localized to mitochondria. 2. cds6 knockout impairs mitochondrial PG and abolishes CL accumulation. 3. Loss of CDS6 causes severe growth defects and plant death.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant analysis; lipid analysis; subcellular localization inference from Tam41 homology and reported study
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for mitochondrial CL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth defect and lethality when disrupted
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike integral membrane CDSs, Tam41-type CDS is a peripheral mitochondrial inner membrane protein

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the phosphatidyl group from CDP-diacylglycerol to glycerol-3-phosphate to form phosphatidylglycerophosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for thylakoid development, photosynthesis, and organelle membrane function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major PGPS isoform required for PG accumulation; loss causes severe PG deficiency, pale leaves, photosynthetic impairment, thylakoid defects, and seedling lethality
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGP1 is a functional PGPS dually localized to chloroplasts and mitochondria. 2. Partial-loss mutant pgp1-1 reduces PG and photosynthetic activity. 3. Null mutants cause drastic PG depletion, defective thylakoid development, severe growth defects, and seedling lethality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (EMS and T-DNA alleles); GFP localization; enzyme activity assays; radiolabeling/lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for plastid PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong loss leads to chloroplast dysfunction, growth arrest, and lethality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SQDG deficiency exacerbates pgp1 phenotypes, indicating partial anionic-lipid compensation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts CDP-diacylglycerol and glycerol-3-phosphate to phosphatidylglycerophosphate in the ER
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ER and extraplastidic membranes; contributes to seed development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports PG biosynthesis redundantly with PGP1, especially during embryogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutant shows little phenotype, but combined loss with PGP1 abolishes PG synthesis activity and severely impairs embryogenesis and organelle development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGP2 is an ER-localized functional PGPS in Arabidopsis. 2. pgp2 single mutants are near wild type. 3. pgp1 pgp2 double mutants lose detectable PG synthesis activity and show severe embryogenesis defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RFP localization; mutant analysis; radiolabeling of PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redundant loss with PGP1 causes embryogenesis defects, no chloroplast development, enlarged mitochondria
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: May contribute to ER-localized PG synthesis and possibly mitochondrial CL pathway through inter-organelle precursor trafficking

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for chloroplast development, photosynthetic activity, and root mitochondrial PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Main PGPP in Arabidopsis; mutants accumulate PGP, lose PG, and show pale tissues, short roots, reduced chlorophyll, and reduced photosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially in hypocotyls, vasculature, trichomes, guard cells, and stigmas
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGPP1 is a HAD-like phosphatase functioning as Arabidopsis PGPP. 2. It complements yeast gep4 and shows PGPP activity in vitro. 3. pgpp1 mutants accumulate PGP, reduce PG, and exhibit chloroplast and root defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; recombinant enzyme assay; GFP/GUS localization; T-DNA mutant analysis; lipidomics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major terminal step enzyme for plastid PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes pale phenotype, short roots, reduced photosynthetic activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual PG in pgpp1 mutants indicates additional phosphatase(s) likely contribute

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein tyrosine phosphatase localized to mitochondrion homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes redundantly to root PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutants show no obvious phenotype, but together with PTPMT2 contribute redundantly with PGPP1 to root PG biosynthesis and root growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTPMT1 is a mammalian-type PGPP homolog in Arabidopsis. 2. It complements yeast gep4. 3. Functional contribution is redundant and most evident in combination with pgpp1 and ptpmt2 backgrounds for root PG homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; mutant genetic interaction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact localization remains unclear; likely not the chloroplast PGPP

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein tyrosine phosphatase localized to mitochondrion homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes redundantly to root PG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single mutants show no obvious phenotype, but ptpmt2 intensifies root-length and PG defects in pgpp1 background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTPMT2 is a second Arabidopsis PTPMT homolog with PGPP activity by complementation. 2. Its function is redundant under normal growth. 3. In combination with pgpp1, it contributes to root PG accumulation and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast complementation; mutant genetic interaction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Localization unresolved; not required for chloroplast PG synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a phosphatidyl group from CDP-diacylglycerol to phosphatidylglycerol to form cardiolipin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for mitochondrial structure, respiratory supercomplex stability, stress tolerance, and normal growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for CL accumulation; mutants show embryo and seedling growth defects, altered mitochondria, reduced respiratory supercomplexes, and stress sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4g04870 encodes Arabidopsis CLS1, the authentic mitochondrial cardiolipin synthase. 2. CLS1 uses CDP-DAG and PG and localizes to mitochondria. 3. cls mutants have strongly reduced or abolished CL and severe mitochondrial and developmental defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous complementation in E. coli; enzymatic characterization; mutant analysis; lipid measurements
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential terminal determinant of cardiolipin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth retardation, low seed yield, abnormal mitochondria, increased stress susceptibility when impaired
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Requires divalent cations and CL-enriched phospholipid environment for optimal activity

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PG catabolism linked to seed TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the sn-1 acyl chain of PG and MGDG, preferentially removing unsaturated acyl groups from phospholipids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage oil for seed development and germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mobilizes plastid lipid acyl groups, including PG-derived acyl chains, for export to ER and incorporation into seed TAG; disruption reduces seed oil and seed weight
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLIP1 is a thylakoid-associated phospholipase A1 that hydrolyzes PG/MGDG. 2. It supports export of plastid-derived acyl groups for seed TAG biosynthesis. 3. plip1 mutants reduce seed oil and overexpression alters plastid and ER lipid balance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assay; mutant and overexpression analysis; metabolic flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces vegetative growth and plastid lipids; loss reduces seed oil and seed weight
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely recognizes 16:1(Δ3t)-containing PG pool in plastids

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes plastid glycerolipids to release acyl groups feeding oxylipin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to ABA-induced oxylipin accumulation; overexpression increases oxylipins and reduces growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLIP2 is a plastid phospholipase A1 homolog of PLIP1. 2. Overexpression increases oxylipin accumulation and reduces vegetative growth. 3. Together with PLIP1/3 it is required for ABA-induced jasmonate-related lipid remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and mutant analysis; hormone treatment phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in hormone-responsive plastid lipid turnover rather than the main PG biosynthetic route

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes plastid glycerolipids to release acyl groups for oxylipin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with PLIP2 to ABA-induced oxylipin and jasmonate accumulation; overexpression reduces growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLIP3 is a plastid phospholipase A1 homolog. 2. Overexpression causes lipid remodeling similar to PLIP1/2 and growth reduction. 3. Together with PLIP1/2 it is necessary for ABA-induced jasmonate-related responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and mutant analysis; ABA treatment assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Participates in hormone-responsive lipid turnover rather than core PG synthesis
